title: pavetta abyssinica subsp. abyssinica
intro: Pavetta abyssinica subsp. abyssinica, also known as Abyssinian wild tea, is a plant species belonging to the family Rubiaceae. It is native to Ethiopia and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The plant is valued for its health benefits, which include reducing fever, relieving headaches, and treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Traditionally, the leaves of Pavetta abyssinica subsp. abyssinica are prepared as a tea by boiling them in water, and the herbal preparation is also used to make infusions and tinctures that can be taken orally to harness its medicinal properties.
